target.name: Polar Semiconductor
target.founder: Independent spinout of Control Data (2024 reformation)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $700M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Polar Semiconductor is a Growth-stage semiconductor company and independent spinout of Control Data, reconstituted in 2024. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers across Chips & Semiconductors: 3 public comps (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private firms including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, and Etched.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Using the company's $700M total capital raised as a proxy for current value, a 1.2x multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement yields $840M. This method is defensible given the target's demonstrated funding velocity and stage position, though relies on the raised-capital proxy absent disclosed valuation.

Peer-median analysis is inapplicable due to insufficient comparable data points within narrow stage and tier filters (0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage peers identified across the semiconductor comp set). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied without target revenue, a standard constraint with private-company analysis. The semiconductor sector's public median of 24.2x P/S across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD is noted but cannot be applied here.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $714M to $966M, with a median point of $840M. Key uncertainties include the absence of disclosed revenue, which precludes cross-validation through public multiples, and limited same-stage peer comps in the narrowly-defined frontier semiconductor space. A second valuation method would strengthen conviction.
